predictions.description: An If whose comparator the engine does not implement ('isNull') FAILS the step — it does not quietly answer false. The witness is that the ELSE branch is absent from the crumbs too: a false condition would have run it, so 'else-ran' is exactly the crumb the old silent-false behavior produced. Neither 'then-ran' nor 'else-ran' appears; the error handler catches the failure and abandons its remaining children ('in-handler-after' skipped); the run continues at the handler's sibling.
The bad comparator raises a real step error — the enclosing error-handler's statusVariable flips to true (it is initialized to false on entry).
The failure message names the offending comparator, so an author can see WHICH comparator is unsupported rather than debugging a branch that mysteriously never runs.
